description: Flag a theorem as an operational bridge if its proof directly uses premises from >=2 distinct PURE clusters (dominant module >=50%); score = number of distinct pure clusters spanned.
motivation: Operationalize 'bridge theorem' via shared premises across domains.
formal_results: 280 bridge theorems (max 3 pure clusters spanned); e.g. Cardinal.sum_lt_prod (AlgebraicGeometry/CategoryTheory/SetTheory), GromovHausdorff.ghDist_le_hausdorffDist.
results_description: 280 theorems mechanically span >=2 pure domains via their premises.
conclusions: Operational bridges are detectable via cross-cluster premises, but the raw 280 are dominated by theorems that merely use ubiquitous foundational lemmas (utility connections), not genuine cross-domain links.
